definitions.text: Not accompanied by anything else; one in number.
examples: Can you give me a single reason not to leave right now?
The vase contained a single long-stemmed rose.
The potatoes left the spoon and landed in a single big lump on the plate.

definitions.text: Designed for the use of only one.
A 45 RPM vinyl record with one song on side A and one on side B.
A popular song released and sold (on any format) nominally on its own though usually having at least one extra track.
examples: The Offspring released four singles from their most recent album.

definitions.text: To get a hit that advances the batter exactly one base.
examples: Pedro singled in the bottom of the eighth inning, which, if converted to a run, would put the team back into contention.

definitions.text: To take the irregular gait called singlefoot.
